title: Sae Far Awa.
content:
Tune - "Dalkeith Maiden Bridge."


I.

        O, sad and heavy should I part,
            But for her sake sae far awa;
        Unknowing what my way may thwart,
            My native land sae far awa.
        Thou that of a' things Maker art,
            That form'd this fair sae far awa,
        Gie body strength, then I'll ne'er start
            At this my way sae far awa.

II.

        How true is love to pure desert,
            So love to her, sae far awa:
        And nocht can heal my bosom's smart,
            While, oh! she is sae far awa.
        Nane other love, nane other dart,
            I feel but hers, sae far awa;
        But fairer never touch'd a heart
            Than hers, the fair sae far awa.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "Sae Far Awa."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
